programmes.show_description: Paramedics treat a man having an anaphylactic reaction, a patient who's having a heart attack and a man receiving chemotherapy who may have developed sepsis.
Gary, 47, has been flown to King's by air ambulance after his motorbike hit a car. Steve, 69, has got a fish bone stuck in his finger while trying to impress a female friend.
A touching episode about people living on their own and reaching out to others. Scaffolder Thomas has been hit on the head by a six-metre pole on a building site.
On day three in Porto, grandmum from Brum Michelle plans a day at the beach, but somehow ends up taking her fellow holidaymakers trekking high up in the mountains. (Ep8/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Edinburgh. The first host, Canadian comedian Larah, plans a night of fine dining. Can an entertaining evening featuring wonderful wigs and celebrity selfies bag her the grand? (Ep1/5)
Edinburgh. Café owner Nutan hopes to impress her guests with her happy-go-lucky attitude and authentic north Indian menu. But eagle-eyed Sindiso scrutinises her food. (Ep2/5)
Edinburgh. South African-born family man Sindiso hosts the third night. Can his spicy chakalaka make up for the criticism he dished out earlier in the week? (Ep3/5)
Edinburgh. Security company manager Anne plans to calm the simmering tension with a homely, relaxed atmosphere, on a night featuring strange collections and weird celebrity crushes. (Ep4/5)
Edinburgh. Pilot James hopes that a touch of sophistication in his high-flying menu will land him safely in first place. But can he keep the peace between his guests? (Ep5/5)
Kay and Barry have decided that it's time to fulfil their dream of buying a holiday home in the historic Spanish city of Jerez. Can Jasmine Harman find their perfect place for their £105k?
With retirement approaching, Lesley and Ken have downsized their UK house to buy a holiday home on the Caribbean island of Barbados. Craig Rowe shows them what their £350k can buy.
Lian and Dave want a holiday home in Spain's Almería Province. Ben Hillman shows them five impressive properties for their £80,000 budget, but are any of them the perfect place?
Motorbike enthusiast Jennie is looking for a holiday home on the western Costa del Sol. With a budget of £85k and Jennie's best friend in tow, can Laura Hamilton find the perfect place?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Lindleys, who are torn over their bungalow in Ashtead, Surrey. Pippa dreams of moving to Kent but Bill's having none of it.
In Reading, Kirstie and Phil help a dad and daughter trawl the trendy suburb of Caversham. And can teachers Richard and Jo find a family home before the school term starts?
...Renovations: George Clarke meets rotten relic renovators. In Yorkshire, Dawn transforms an old village shop into a luxurious home with an outdoor oasis.
The first visit is to Sherbourne House in Norfolk. Pauline and Dave hope that their hospitality experience will give them the edge, but the rooms prove too hot for some guests to handle.
Musical theatre lovers Lez and Derek hope to dazzle their guests with a showstopping stay. But cutting comments about the glassware and some surprisingly low scores cause drama for the duo.
The B&Bers visit The Greyhound Hotel in Cromford, Derbyshire. Tina and Danielle blow their guests away with the comfy beds, but a trickle of traffic leads to some rude awakenings.
The final visit is to The Royal Hotel in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex. Vicki and Kara's boutique seafront hotel wows the guests, but talk turns tense with accusations of foul play...
The B&Bers meet for the last time to settle some scores. There's beef about breakfast and tabletop troubles, and Derek discusses his slumber sorrows. Which B&B has won the week?
Brand new series: Paramedics treat a young woman who has passed out for the third time that day and a man in his 40s with chest pain and a family history of heart failure.
Mick is rushed to Queen's with suspected sepsis. Keith arrives with one of the highest heart rates the department has ever seen. And Jillian has had a fall at a charity event.
In Nottingham, staff at Queen's Medical Centre deal with a crime wave of needle-spiking attacks on female university students, including Kate, who collapsed at a nightclub.
Brand new series: Paramedics treat a young woman who has passed out for the third time that day and a man in his 40s with chest pain and a family history of heart failure.
Mick is rushed to Queen's with suspected sepsis. Keith arrives with one of the highest heart rates the department has ever seen. And Jillian has had a fall at a charity event.
In Nottingham, staff at Queen's Medical Centre deal with a crime wave of needle-spiking attacks on female university students, including Kate, who collapsed at a nightclub.
On the fourth day in Porto, culture vulture Shabaz leads a historical tour by tuk-tuk, but will his evening of melancholy music give his fellow holidaymakers the blues? (Ep9/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The first host, Mrs Tee, charms her guests with her eccentric ways, at the four-star Gorse Meadow in the New Forest. But not everyone's room is up to scratch.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Suffolk, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet former agony aunt Janet, whose garden shed is crammed with goodies, including some rare furniture and a vintage tram bench.
Andi and Neil dream of retiring to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Can Laura Hamilton find their perfect place close to the coastal town of Mazarrón, near Murcia, for £145,000?
Belfast area. Chatty PR manager Claire plans a menu of her restaurant favourites. But she has a challenge on her hands, as she's never plucked a pheasant before... (Ep1/5)
Belfast area. Carpenter Neil takes his guests on a weird and wonderful visit to the countryside, featuring country-style cuisine, farmyard animals and a 'head in a jar'. (Ep2/5)
Belfast area. Debonair window-cleaning business owner Paul plans an evening of fancy food and fine dining. For some of his guests, it's uncharted territory. (Ep3/5)
Belfast area. DJ and events organiser Ryan hosts an evening of fun, with a hipster band playing in his living room and several crises in the kitchen. (Ep4/5)
Belfast area. Final host Cailin throws a Eurovision extravaganza, with dishes from across Europe and full-on fancy dress. Can she walk away with the money, money, money? (Ep5/5)
The first visit is to The Swan Inn in Midhurst, West Sussex, run by Digby Furneaux. A sunny breakfast satisfies their guests, but Cleide feels rather exposed by the bathroom window.
Sunny and Faraah host at Osterley Park Hotel in Isleworth. Unfortunately, all of the guests complain about a cleaning catastrophe, and Digby checks out early.
Cleide Brook hosts at Plover Cottage in Huddersfield. The guests are amazed by the space, but Liz and Gary find more than they bargained for in their room, leading to some rude feedback.
The last visit is to The Forge Guest Rooms in Long Whatton, Leicestershire, run by Liz and Gary Scottorn. The guests love the luxury, but Cleide questions eating breakfast beside a hot tub.
The guests meet for the last time to find out how much they've been paid. Criticisms are laid bare as Digby reveals how much he paid for a room he didn't even spend the night in.
Peugeot 106 Rallye: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a 1997 French hot hatch, owned by 21-year-old Senna. Can they bring some much-needed joy to Senna's life?
Alpine A110: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up one of France's most legendary rally cars. Guido has owned it for 20 years but never driven it.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of first-time buyers who are struggling to find homes in Southampton and the New Forest.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Larne, Kirstie and Phil help Catherine and Nigel decide whether to ditch their rural 70s bungalow or stick with it. Could a garage hold the key?
...Clive and Kids: It's summer and a huge landmark is reached: Anty John's has electric light for the first time ever. And Amanda reflects on an emotional year.
This episode focuses on how people deal with physical and emotional pain. Dad-of-four Simon hasn't eaten for days after his partner ended their relationship.
A look at the challenges of motherhood and the responsibility of raising sons, meeting mothers with concerns about their own health and that of their babies and adult sons.
